etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "tree") with flames -> sacrifice with firewood -> 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") added for emphasis -> 呂(呂) (lǚ) used as phonetic
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a tree encircled by flames, symbolizing a sacrifice using firewood. Later, 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") was added to emphasize the element of fire. 呂(呂) (lǚ) was then introduced as a phonetic component, linking it to characters like 寮 and 潦.
